steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses: 'the guide soon grew in scope and ______' and 'its modest French eateries were replaced...'. Two independent clauses must be joined with a comma plus a coordinating conjunction (FANBOYS). The word before the blank ends the first clause; the blank must supply the missing connector.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — influence,: A comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — influence and: A coordinating conjunction alone, with no comma, between two independent clauses is a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — influence, and: Comma + coordinating conjunction correctly joins the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice D — influence: No punctuation or conjunction at all — a fused sentence. ✗
That matches Choice C — influence, and.
